data.title: Rezolve AI gains as Roth MKM starts with Buy rating
data.description:
• Rezolve AI (NASDAQ:RZLV) shares gained 4% in premarket trading on Tuesday as research firm Roth MKM started coverage on the generative AI company with a Buy rating and $4 price target.
  • "RZLV's AI solutions transform the e-commerce sales process to mimic an in- store interaction with a trained salesperson," Roth MKM analyst Rohit Kulkarni wrote in a note to clients. "These AI products utilize agentic tech to simplify how a user searches, compares, interacts with customer service, and performs post-sale inquiries. Commerce platforms and retailers work with RZLV to include chatbots and conversational workflows in all aspects of marketing, commerce, customer service, and payments."
  • In addition, the recent deals with Microsoft (MSFT) and Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) "increase credibility and provide [near-term] conviction," Kulkarni added. "Our diligence calls with MSFT plus LLM benchmarking increase our confidence in RZLV's near-term trajectory."

data.title: Q3 labor costs revised down, while productivity number stays at 2.2%
data.description:
• Q3 Productivity and Costs: Nonfarm productivity: +2.2% vs. +2.2% consensus and +2.2% preliminary estimate and 2.5% in Q2.
  • Unit labor costs: +0.8% vs. +1.9% expected and +1.9% prior estimate and +0.4% in Q2.
  • Breaking... check back for updates.

data.title: Nano Labs buys bitcoins worth $5.5M
data.description:
• Nano Labs (NASDAQ:NA) said it has invested in bitcoins (BTC-USD), and now owns ~55.6 BTC, valued at about $5.5 million as of December 10.
  • The firm purchased bitcoin at an average price of ~$99,400 per BTC.
  • This comes after the company has said earlier in December that it plans to purchase and hold $50 million worth of bitcoin (BTC-USD) over the next five years.

data.title: Applied Optoelectronics cut by B. Riley on elevated valuation
data.description:
Applied Optoelectronics (NASDAQ:AAOI) was in the spotlight on Tuesday as investment firm B. Riley downgraded the telecom equipment maker, citing an elevated valuation in shares.

Applied Optoelectronics fell 7.5% in premarket trading, while Lumentum (NASDAQ:LITE), which was also called out — but not downgraded — was up fractionally.

"We are downgrading Applied Opto ... from Neutral to Sell, as our latest checks indicate 400G could be decelerating," analyst Dave Kang wrote in a note to clients. Applied Optoelectronics and Lumentum shares have done "extremely well" in the second-half, "perhaps too well, in our view," Kang added, stating the pair have "significantly outperformed" competitors Coherent (COHR) and Fabrinet (FN) since the election.

"We believe this discrepancy should eliminate the idea that U.S. optical companies are potentially benefiting from the new administration's hawkish stance against Chinese companies since shares of COHR and FN, both U.S. companies, have badly lagged LITE and AAOI in recent months," Kang continued. "Rather, we would argue the discrepancy between them supports our thesis of the unbundling of NVIDIA's (NVDA) platforms, which led to our recent downgrade of FN to Sell."

Delving deeper, Lumentum recently announced a second deal with a hyperscaler for its 800G products, following its deal with Google. And while Applied Optoelectronics also said it expects to ship 800G products this quarter, Kang said that "should be taken with some skepticism given an inconsistent track record."

As such, recent checks into 400G spending indicated a possible deceleration for Applied Optoelectronics, which could negatively impact the company's ability to hit $100M in quarterly revenue run rate, Kang posited. "With AAOI's 800G yet to be qualified by any hyperscalers, we don't believe they can sustain a $100M quarterly run-rate on CATV alone, leading us to downgrade to a Sell rating."

data.title: Sirius XM names Wayne Thorsen as COO
data.description:
• Sirius XM (NASDAQ:SIRI) said it has appointed Wayne Thorsen as its COO and executive vice president, effective December 16.
  • Thorsen most recently served as the chief business officer at ADT.
  • In the newly-created role of COO at SIRI, Thorsen will oversee its product and technology functions and be responsible for aspects of the company's commercial activities, business development, and corporate strategy.
  • The firm also said Joseph Inzerillo has stepped down from his role as chief product and technology officer, effective immediately.

data.title: Celularity regains compliance with Nasdaq listing rule
data.description:
• Celularity (NASDAQ:CELU) has regained compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5250(c)(1) after the company filed its Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the period ended Sept 30, 2024.
  • The filing delays were primarily the result of challenges associated with Celularity’s efforts to address a backlog of prior filings, including the Forms 10-Q for the first and second quarters of 2024, which were filed recently.
  • CELU rose 3.04% premarket on Tuesday.
